title: Dev Container stuck indefinitely during build on podman
body:
### Issue Description

devcontainer build initiated through ssh from vscode gets stuck indefinitely.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Workflow in vscode
"Connect to remote Development Workspaces" >  "[Git clone repo](https://github.com/codeblue122/velo-python)" after ssh through vscode 
2. To over come following error in initial build, 
~~~
mkdir: cannot create directory '/home/vscode/.vscode-server': Read-only file system
~~~
I have added  to container.json
~~~
		"--userns=keep-id",
		"--group-add=keep-groups",
		"--security-opt=label=disable",
		"--mount", "type=tmpfs,destination=/home/vscode/.vscode-server",
		"--mount", "type=bind,source=${localWorkspaceFolder},target=/workspace:Z"
~~~

Not sure if all options needed or other better way.

3. in VScode "rebuild and reopen devcontainer"


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

Build stuck indefinitely at 
~~~
Using cached extensions scan result system file:///vscode/vscode-server/bin/linux-x64/ddc367ed5c8936efe395cffeec279b04ffd7db78/extensions
[37565 ms] Killed
[76727 ms] Start: Run in container: mkdir -p '/vscode/vscode-server/extensionsCache' && cd '/home/vscode/.vscode-server/extensionsCache' && cp 'github.copilot-1.297.0.sigzip' '/vscode/vscode-server/extensionsCache'
[76770 ms] 
[76770 ms] 
[76770 ms] Start: Run in container: cd '/vscode/vscode-server/extensionsCache' && ls -t | tail -n +500 | xargs rm -f
~~~

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

Expected to build and provide container access.

### podman info output

```yaml
Remote 
Automotive Stream Distribution release 9 (running on Qemu)
rpm -q podman
podman-4.9.2-1.el9.x86_64

Local Fedora
Fedora release 39 (Thirty Nine)
code-1.98.2-1741788968.el8.x86_64
Devcontainers Extension 0.401.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Remote OS on qemu
auto-osbuild-qemu-autosd9-developer-regular
(https://autosd.sig.centos.org/AutoSD-9/nightly/sample-images/)

If running on macOS Instructions at bottom
https://sig.centos.org/automotive/getting-started/getting-started-on-macos/#quick-start-building-customized-autosd-images-in-a-qemu-vm


### Additional information

Current logs

[velocitas_devcontainer.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19654189/velocitas_devcontainer.log)

comments.body:

I wonder if you are hitting https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/171

Hi @giuseppe, thanks for pointing these.
It didnt work on current setup so did a fresh setup, still same issues.
Following are things I tried.

<details>
    <summary>driver related changes</summary>

[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
transient_store = false

[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = []

[storage.options.pull_options]
enable_partial_images = "false"
use_hard_links = "false"
ostree_repos = ""

[storage.options.overlay]
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=off"

[storage.options.thinpool]

</details>

2] Error from 

<details>
    <summary>podman logs --latest</summary>

Container started
mount: /sys/kernel/security: permission denied.
Could not mount /sys/kernel/security.

3] It is still stuck at
[38581 ms] [05:49:30] Extension 'ms-python.python' v2025.4.0 was successfully installed.
[38581 ms] [05:49:30] Extension 'ms-python.vscode-pylance' v2025.4.1 was successfully installed.
[38583 ms] [05:49:30] No extensions are marked as removed.

Also tried removing :Z and use just "--security-opt=label=disable" But no luck.

@codeblue122 I could setup a working environment. Here is what I have done:
- I have booted an autosd VM as documented [here](https://sig.centos.org/automotive/getting-started/getting-started-on-macos/#quick-start-booting-prebuilt-autosd-images-in-a-qemu-vm)
- I logged in as `root` and I have created the files `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`, both with identical content:
    ```
    guest:200000:65536
    ```
- I logged in as `guest` and I have created the file `/home/guest/.config/containers/containers.conf`:
    ```conf
    [engine]
    read_only = false
    events_logger = "file"
    cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
    ```
- I have created a [velo-python GitHub repository](https://github.com/l0rd/velo-python/) from the [template](https://github.com/eclipse-velocitas/vehicle-app-python-template)
- I have used VS Code to connect via SSH to the VM **as user guest** , cloned the `velo-python` repository and re-opened it in a container.

The startup works fine with no error and I am able to edit files. 

I have seen some `Out of memory` errors that may get fixed increasing the VM memory size.

```
[  391.468698] Out of memory: Killed process 4826 (node) total-vm:27708132kB, anon-rss:885492kB, file-rss:384kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:17056kB oom_score_adj:0
[  399.703967] Out of memory: Killed process 5705 (node) total-vm:26763536kB, anon-rss:590144kB, file-rss:1536kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:10676kB oom_score_adj:0
[  405.566138] Out of memory: Killed process 6161 (node) total-vm:26761360kB, anon-rss:588836kB, file-rss:128kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:10624kB oom_score_adj:0
```

Let me know if that works for you as well. I may have forgot some steps.

Thanks!
I didn't get OOM, have enough memory.
Followed the steps as mentioned cross checked but still issue.

` EACCES: permission denied, open '/root/.local/share/velocitas/package.json'
`
The container runs but tasks fails due to readonly workspace, 
also socket of the container fail with error. 

( Between this container lets build locally as root user on poman. )

Something that I forgot to mention is that I used the pre-release version of dev containers VS Code extension that has some important updates:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e03060f5-b02c-41cc-b1b9-fadfd0112c0b)

If that doesn't work for you, please share the devcontainers log as a pastebin/gist.

Updated the vscode and then Dev Container versions to said pre release,
but interestingly it is failing at different location now.

https://gist.github.com/codeblue122/4aac2d9a11598aeb1688d95ee3921ca9

Had removed .vscode-server and .vscode for guest user, just in case
The last error is `no space left on device: exit status 1`. You can try to `podman system prune --all` or increasing the VM disk size.

Container run with writable workspace now.
But remote host the sample application in SDK does not run correctly, 
with complain about docker.

~~~
>>>> Start log of mqtt-broker >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>
Starting 'mqtt-broker'
docker run --rm --init --name mqtt-broker -p 1883:1883 -p 9001:9001 --network host eclipse-mosquitto:2.0.14 mosquitto -c /mosquitto-no-auth.conf

docker: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?.
~~~

Same runs on my local host with podman.

Steps to run.
1] Ctrl + F1 > Run Task > Import Example app from SDK
2] Ctrl + F1 > Run Task > Local Runtime Up
3] Ctrl + F1 > Run Task > Local Runtime - Run Vehicle APP

For [details](https://github.com/eclipse-velocitas/vehicle-app-python-sdk/tree/main/examples/seat-adjuster) on running app

Hi @l0rd 
Thanks for your continued help of this issue, we have almost run the containers except the last one.

I have tried to 
- stop podman service as root and started as guest user.
- have restarted Dev Container with `"-v", "/run/user/2000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"`
- Manually created `/home/vscode/` and given it all worldly permissions

`Local Runtime Up` task which still seems to fail.
but with ``"-v", "/run/user/2000/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock"`` manually running 
`docker run --rm --init --name mqtt-broker -p 1883:1883 -p 9001:9001 --network host eclipse-mosquitto:2.0.14 mosquitto -c /mosquitto-no-auth.con` works, but the third task still fails. ` Local Runtime - Run Vehicle APP`

Would appreciate your push on correct system configuration, procedure.

@codeblue122 to have access to the Podman socket you need to:
1) Enable `podman.socket` in the VM: run, as guest user, `systemctl --user start podman.socket` (read more about it [here](https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.5.0/markdown/podman-system-service.1.html))
2) [Mount the podman socket in the Dev Container](https://github.com/l0rd/velo-python/commit/924fbf9bd3405a8c497557d49bb4f42d24ec2b5a)

After that, `docker ps` works fine from within the Dev Container so the tasks should work too. But I wasn't able to test them because VS Code remote extension got killed with OOM errors 🤷 :
```
[  135.288246] Out of memory: Killed process 2567 (node) total-vm:23557560kB, anon-rss:586352kB, file-rss:768kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:12084kB oom_score_adj:0
[  140.998427] Out of memory: Killed process 3030 (node) total-vm:22815716kB, anon-rss:506088kB, file-rss:1536kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:9872kB oom_score_adj:0
[  166.430086] Out of memory: Killed process 3477 (node) total-vm:22817452kB, anon-rss:513192kB, file-rss:384kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:9996kB oom_score_adj:0
[  320.093585] Out of memory: Killed process 6428 (node) total-vm:23683688kB, anon-rss:708680kB, file-rss:768kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:15144kB oom_score_adj:0
[  326.380746] Out of memory: Killed process 7311 (node) total-vm:23197404kB, anon-rss:565560kB, file-rss:384kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:11360kB oom_score_adj:0
[  331.398966] Out of memory: Killed process 7765 (node) total-vm:22792336kB, anon-rss:490956kB, file-rss:128kB, shmem-rss:0kB, UID:2000 pgtables:9716kB oom_score_adj:0
```

I have increased the memory by starting the VM with the command `/automotive-image-builder/automotive-image-runner --verbose --nographics --memory=8G ./autosd9-developer-regular-aarch64.qcow2`.

I have tried to run the task `Local Runtime Up`:
1. It failed to pull the image `ghcr.io/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databroker:0.5.0`. So I have `podman pull ghcr.io/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databroker:0.5.0` as the guest user in the VM. 
2. It failed again with error `mkdir /home/vscode: permission denied.`. This is the command `velocitas exec runtime-local up`. The workaround here is to create a symlink as root in the VM `ln -s -f /home/guest /home/vscode && chown -h guest:guest /home/vscode` (but it would be better to use a flag or something to specify a different `HOME`)
3. It continues to fail:
```
docker run --rm --init --name vehicledatabroker -e KUKSA_DATABROKER_METADATA_FILE=/home/vscode/.velocitas/projects/4ae0c3afc1478745dcd5ed36586c200a/vspec.json -e KUKSA_DATABROKER_PORT=55555 -v /home/vscode/.velocitas/projects/4ae0c3afc1478745dcd5ed36586c200a/vspec.json:/home/vscode/.velocitas/projects/4ae0c3afc1478745dcd5ed36586c200a/vspec.json --network host ghcr.io/eclipse-kuksa/kuksa-databroker:0.5.0 --enable-databroker-v1
2025-05-19T17:21:31.588637Z  INFO databroker: Init logging from RUST_LOG (environment variable not found)
2025-05-19T17:21:31.588706Z  INFO databroker: Starting Kuksa Databroker 0.5.0
2025-05-19T17:21:31.588713Z  INFO databroker: Using 8 threads with 8 cores available on the system
2025-05-19T17:21:31.589262Z  INFO databroker: Populating metadata from file '/home/vscode/.velocitas/projects/4ae0c3afc1478745dcd5ed36586c200a/vspec.json'
Error: Os { code: 13, kind: PermissionDenied, message: "Permission denied" }
```
I don't know what's going on here. Maybe you can figure out what's the operation that is failing?

@codeblue122, can we close this issue? At this point, there isn't much we can do from a Podman point of view. And anyway, the problem described in the title/subject of the issue has been addressed and keeping it open as is is confusing.
@l0rd Yes we have moved ahead from original issue, should close this one, will open a new one for latest error with recent findings. Thanks for all the investigation on this one.
